label: Introduction
description: A member of Congress writes the bill and submits it to the House or Senate clerk. It gets a number and is referred to a committee.

label: Committee
description: The committee studies the bill, holds hearings, marks it up, and votes on whether to send it to the full chamber. Most bills die here.

label: House vote
description: The full House debates and votes. A majority is required to pass.

label: Senate vote
description: The Senate goes through its own version of the process. Differences between the two chambers must be reconciled.

label: President
description: The president signs the bill into law, vetoes it, or lets it become law without a signature.
